description: SQL extraction coverage: verifies the A1.1 SQL deep extractor identifies SQL patterns across DDL, DML, JPA, PreparedStatement, stored procedures, and JPQL in the network-iq-backend-java codebase.

examples.input:
CREATE TABLE network_provider (
    provider_id BIGINT PRIMARY KEY GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY,
    npi_number VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    provider_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    specialty_code VARCHAR(20),
    network_tier VARCHAR(10) CHECK (network_tier IN ('TIER1','TIER2','TIER3')),
    effective_date DATE NOT NULL,
    termination_date DATE,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT now()
);
examples.expected: DDL extracted with table name, all column names, types, and constraints

examples.input:
CREATE TABLE claim_line (
    claim_line_id BIGINT PRIMARY KEY GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY,
    claim_id BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES claim(claim_id),
    procedure_code VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL,
    diagnosis_code VARCHAR(10),
    billed_amount NUMERIC(12,2) NOT NULL,
    allowed_amount NUMERIC(12,2),
    paid_amount NUMERIC(12,2),
    service_date DATE NOT NULL
);

SELECT p.provider_id, p.npi_number, p.provider_name, n.network_name, COUNT(c.claim_id) AS claim_count
FROM network_provider p
INNER JOIN network n ON p.network_id = n.network_id
LEFT JOIN claim c ON c.rendering_provider_id = p.provider_id
WHERE p.effective_date <= :effectiveDate
  AND (p.termination_date IS NULL OR p.termination_date > :asOfDate)
  AND n.plan_id = :planId
GROUP BY p.provider_id, p.npi_number, p.provider_name, n.network_name
HAVING COUNT(c.claim_id) > 0
ORDER BY claim_count DESC;
examples.expected: DML SELECT with JOIN, WHERE, GROUP BY, HAVING extracted

examples.input:
SELECT m.member_id, m.member_name, m.date_of_birth,
       e.group_number, e.plan_code, e.benefit_start_date
FROM member m
INNER JOIN enrollment e ON e.member_id = m.member_id
INNER JOIN plan_benefit pb ON pb.plan_code = e.plan_code
WHERE m.ssn_hash = :ssnHash
  AND e.benefit_start_date <= CURRENT_DATE
  AND (e.benefit_end_date IS NULL OR e.benefit_end_date >= CURRENT_DATE)
  AND pb.is_active = true;
examples.expected: DML SELECT with multiple JOINs and date range WHERE clause
JPA @Query annotation string extracted with JPQL
JPA native @Query with BETWEEN and NOT IN extracted

examples.input:
String upsertSql = "INSERT INTO eligibility_snapshot " +
    "(member_id, plan_code, group_number, as_of_date, benefit_data, created_at) " +
    "VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?::jsonb, now()) " +
    "ON CONFLICT (member_id, as_of_date) " +
    "DO UPDATE SET benefit_data = EXCLUDED.benefit_data, " +
    "updated_at = now()";
PreparedStatement ps = conn.prepareStatement(upsertSql);

examples.expected: Stored procedure call with OUT parameter extracted

examples.input:
UPDATE claim
SET claim_status = :newStatus,
    adjudication_date = :adjudicationDate,
    paid_amount = :paidAmount,
    eob_sent = true,
    updated_at = now()
WHERE claim_id = :claimId
  AND claim_status IN ('PENDING', 'IN_REVIEW')
  AND assigned_adjudicator_id = :adjudicatorId;
examples.expected: DML UPDATE with SET and conditional WHERE extracted
